spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates positive, negative, and zero superellipse() numbers, named values, live CSSOM updates, and malformed superellipse() rejection.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now exposes the malformed superellipse(foo) failure path, current spec-accepted negative and zero superellipse() values, and a visible unsupported-browser fallback note.

references.rationale: The see-also section now cites https://chromestatus.com/feature/5357329815699456 and the CSS Borders 4 superellipse() spec section.

design_system.rationale: Static scan found no raw hex values, source inline style attributes, inline handlers, or DOM markup assignment; the local fallback tint uses shared design tokens.

summary: Repaired with Chrome 148 / chrome-devtools-mcp verification. The slider still updates the live superellipse() value and annotation, the new contract panel verifies accepted positive/negative/zero numbers and rejected malformed input, the unsupported-browser note now becomes visible, source inline style issues were removed, and the required ChromeStatus/spec references are present.
